page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance, as measured by stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression, falls into the weak category, representing the bottom 25% of tested systems. However, multi-core workloads leverage the full 32-core allocation effectively, achieving average tier results in stress-ng multi-core tests, bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks remains consistently in the average range, supporting stable data throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads that require substantial parallel processing without needing specialized GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and redis caching, where its average multi-core performance and 10 Gbps network bandwidth can be fully utilized.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core capabilities across stress-ng, PassMark, and bzip3 decompression tests. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the instance a reliable option for parallelized workloads, redis caching, and static web serving where dedicated multi-core execution is required without the need for high-speed local storage.
